description: A thread or filament from which textile is made; the basic unit of woven material. 'Linua' names what becomes cloth—the strand before spinning, the element of weaving.
sound_symbolism: The word lines thin. 'Li' draws with linear extension, fiber lengthening. 'Nu' binds with structural integrity. Then 'a' opens to possibility of cloth.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo linua' (the fiber). Raw material that becomes 'sima' (thread) through spinning. Plant or animal origin.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Fiber sustained covering—cotton, wool, silk, hemp, the raw material of all textile.
pillars.solarpunk-values: Natural fibers—renewable, biodegradable, the sustainable alternative to synthetic.
